text:
RESTAGING CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM

CLINICAL INDICATION: 63-year-old female with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, right lower lobe primary. Disease progressed on osi after 14 months with emergence of acquired EGFR T790M resistance mutation and new intracranial metastases (right cerebellum, left frontal lobe, right parietal lobe). Underwent Gamma Knife SRS to three brain lesions approximately 10 weeks ago. Subsequently initiated second-line Paraplatin/peme, now completed 3 cycles (Paraplatin dose-reduced to AUC 4 after cycle 2 thrombocytopenia). History of mediastinal lymphadenopathy and known right adrenal metastasis. Presenting for restaging evaluation.

TECHNIQUE:
CT: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis obtained following intravenous administration of 100 mL of iohexol contrast. Images reconstructed at 1.25 mm slice thickness in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Lung window, mediastinal window, and bone window reconstructions reviewed.
MRI brain: 3T magnet. Standard sequences performed: T1 pre-contrast, T2, FLAIR, DWI/ADC, susceptibility-weighted imaging (SWI), T1 post-gadolinium (axial, coronal, sagittal). Gadolinium-based contrast agent: gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg IV administered.

COMPARISON:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis dated approximately 5 months prior (time of progression on Tagrisso), at which time the right lower lobe mass measured 3.1 cm and adrenal nodule measured 1.4 cm.
MRI brain obtained at time of CNS progression (approximately 10-11 weeks prior), which showed three enhancing intracranial lesions.

--- CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS ---

FINDINGS:

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:
The right lower lobe mass previously measuring 3.1 cm now measures approximately 2.4 cm in greatest axial dimension. The lesion retains a partially spiculated margin and demonstrates heterogeneous density without cavitation. There is no central necrosis. The reduction in size compared to the prior CT is notable, though the lesion remains conspicuous. No endobronchial component is identified. The adjacent right lower lobe parenchyma shows mild post-obstructive atelectatic changes, somewhat improved compared to prior.

Multiple small bilateral pulmonary nodules were noted on the prior study. The current examination demonstrates these nodules to be stable in size and number. The largest previously measured 8 mm in the right upper lobe and now measures approximately 7 mm - no meaningful change. Left-sided nodules similarly appear stable. No new pulmonary nodules are identified.

The central airways are patent. No endobronchial mass. No mucous plugging. Lungs are otherwise clear. No consolidation, ground-glass opacity, or interstitial thickening to suggest infection, drug toxicity, or radiation pneumonitis in the right lower lobe treatment field.

PLEURAL SPACES:
No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pleural nodularity.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
Previously enlarged right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node measuring 1.2 cm at the time of progression is now decreased, measuring approximately 0.8 cm in short axis. This node remains borderline in size but has diminished compared to the prior study. Subcarinal and other mediastinal nodal stations do not demonstrate pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. The hilar structures appear unremarkable.

HEART AND GREAT VESSELS:
Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta and major branch vessels are normal in caliber without dissection or aneurysmal change. Pulmonary vasculature is unremarkable.

CHEST WALL AND THORACIC SKELETON:
No acute fracture. No focal lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions suspicious for metastatic involvement in the visualized thoracic skeleton, including ribs, thoracic spine, sternum, and clavicles. Surgical clips or implanted hardware: none. No axillary lymphadenopathy.

ABDOMEN:

LIVER:
The liver is normal in size with homogeneous parenchymal attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions are identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

GALLBLADDER AND BILIARY TRACT:
Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No cholelithiasis. Common bile duct within normal limits.

PANCREAS:
The pancreas enhances homogeneously without focal mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic abnormality.

SPLEEN:
Within normal limits in size and attenuation. No focal lesions.

KIDNEYS AND URETERS:
Bilateral kidneys enhance symmetrically. No hydronephrosis. No renal mass or calculus identified. The ureters are not dilated.

ADRENAL GLANDS:
Right adrenal gland: The previously identified right adrenal nodule, which measured 1.4 cm at the time of initial systemic progression and was confirmed as metastatic adenocarcinoma on biopsy, now measures approximately 1.4 cm in greatest dimension and is stable compared to the prior CT. No significant change in attenuation or morphology. Left adrenal gland is normal in appearance with no nodularity.

GASTROINTESTINAL TRACT:
Stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are unremarkable without evidence of obstruction, wall thickening, or mass. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

LYMPH NODES - ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:
No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymph nodes identified.

PELVIS:
Uterus and bilateral adnexa are within normal limits for age. Urinary bladder is unremarkable. No pelvic free fluid. No pelvic osseous lesion.

VASCULATURE:
Abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. Celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery origins are patent. No thrombus identified in the abdominal vasculature. Inferior vena cava is normal.

BONE WINDOWS (ABDOMEN/PELVIS):
No lytic or blastic osseous lesions in the visualized lumbar spine, sacrum, iliac wings, or proximal femora.

--- MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM ---

FINDINGS:

The brain is appropriate in size for age. Gray-white matter differentiation is preserved throughout. The sulci and ventricular system are normal in caliber. No midline shift. No uncal herniation.

SITE 1 - RIGHT CEREBELLAR HEMISPHERE:
Previously identified 1.1 cm enhancing lesion in the right cerebellar hemisphere treated with Gamma Knife SRS (20 Gy single fraction). Currently measures approximately 0.6 cm in greatest dimension with a markedly reduced enhancement footprint. There is central T1 hyperintensity consistent with evolving radiation necrosis/coagulative necrosis. The previously noted surrounding vasogenic edema is substantially reduced. No new surrounding infiltrative signal abnormality.

SITE 2 - LEFT FRONTAL LOBE:
Previously identified 0.8 cm left frontal lobe enhancing lesion, treated with Gamma Knife SRS (22 Gy single fraction). The lesion now measures approximately 0.4 cm. Enhancement is markedly diminished, now appearing as a small focus of residual or post-treatment enhancement without significant edema. T2/FLAIR signal in the surrounding white matter has also decreased substantially. This appearance is consistent with a favorable post-radiosurgical response.

SITE 3 - RIGHT PARIETAL LOBE:
Previously identified 0.6 cm right parietal lobe lesion treated with Gamma Knife SRS (24 Gy single fraction). Currently, this lesion is nearly imperceptible, measuring approximately 0.3 cm with minimal residual enhancement. No surrounding edema. This lesion demonstrates an excellent treatment response.

REST OF BRAIN:
No new enhancing lesions are identified on the post-contrast sequences. No additional foci suspicious for new metastatic deposits, including at the gray-white junction, basal ganglia, or posterior fossa. No leptomeningeal enhancement is seen on post-gadolinium sequences, including along the sulci, cranial nerves, or cerebellar folia.

DWI/ADC: No acute diffusion restriction to suggest recent infarction.

SWI: No significant susceptibility artifact at the lesion sites beyond expected post-treatment hemosiderin deposition. No evidence of large volume hemorrhagic transformation.

SELLA AND SKULL BASE:
Appearance within normal limits. Pituitary gland without mass or abnormal enhancement.

CALVARIUM AND SCALP:
No calvarial metastases. No scalp mass.

--- IMPRESSION ---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS:
1. The right lower lobe primary lung mass has decreased in size from 3.1 cm to approximately 2.4 cm, in keeping with a meaningful response to carbo/Alimta chemotherapy.
2. The previously confirmed right adrenal metastasis is unchanged at 1.4 cm - stable over the current treatment period.
3. Bilateral pulmonary nodules are stable without new lesions developing or existing ones enlarging, suggesting absence of new pulmonary dissemination.
4. Previously enlarged right paratracheal lymph node (station 4R) has diminished from 1.2 cm to 0.8 cm, suggesting a partial nodal response.
5. No new sites of intrathoracic, abdominal, or pelvic metastatic disease identified.
6. No pleural effusion.

MRI BRAIN:
7. All three previously treated intracranial metastases (right cerebellum, left frontal lobe, right parietal lobe) demonstrate substantial volume reduction and evolving post-treatment changes consistent with good responses to stereotactic radiosurgery. No residual aggressive enhancement to suggest viable tumor progression at any treated site.
8. No new intracranial metastases. No leptomeningeal disease.

Overall, imaging findings are compatible with response to second-line chemotherapy in the chest, with stable adrenal disease and an excellent intracranial response to the prior radiosurgery. The dominant finding of concern is stability of the adrenal metastasis; all other sites reflect favorable treatment effect.
